summary: Relocates the check:docs gate script to its canonical .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh, widens artifactSchema to track the installed-artifact contract (canonical file locations, not just project.json), settles the deferred PR #17 ledger entries, adds the MATERIA.md § Design tool section (the design-tool capability seam, default none), lands the human design-review gate, rebuilds the design stage around canvas authoring (paired artifact, assertions, gate-arrival sync, architecture bounce), lands the committed design-snapshot export (directory contract, git-ignore guard, README template, and the ship-spec gate's commit/sync/presentation mechanics), and lands the static design-conformance review angle with its deterministic § Eyes conformance harness (the gated seventh review angle, a finding-schema classification field, § Loop dispositions, the § Review behavioral-verify-lane wiring, and the § Course corrections design-debt banner).

changes.summary: The binding check:docs gate script (.materia/scripts/check-docs.sh) must be present; a repo predating it fails the gate.
changes.manualMigration: Copy the gate script from the installed plugin scaffold (scaffold/.materia/scripts/check-docs.sh) to .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh, and remove any stale scripts/check-docs.mjs (the artifact the .sh replaced) if present. Then update any consumer references that still name the old checker: the MATERIA.md § Gate row, package scripts (package.json), CI workflows, settings allowlists, and present-state docs (the dated run folders under docs/specs/ and docs/bugs/_reports/ are frozen and exempt). Or run /materia:migrate --apply, which installs it via the install-check-docs migration and surfaces those stale references (referenceFollowUps) for its skill to sweep before re-running the check:docs gate.
changes.notes: Historical drift, recorded now at release time: the check:docs gate script originated before PR #10 as scripts/check-docs.mjs, which the portable POSIX-sh check-docs.sh later replaced; a pre-#10 dogfood repo has neither at the canonical location and fails the binding gate. Classified required (operator decision): the fix is mechanical and one-command. This entry's schema-3 window (1,3] reaches BOTH untracked-legacy and schema-2 installs, so a missing gate yields action-needed uniformly regardless of tracking vintage; the adopted-drift filter spares any repo that already carries the script (check-docs-sh-present ok). Renamed from PR #17's suggested id 0.2.0-check-docs-sh: it lives in 0.3.0.json (0.2.0.json, a shipped file, is never retro-edited) so the window reaches untracked repos too.

changes.summary: The check:docs gate script's canonical location moved from scripts/check-docs.sh to .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh.
changes.manualMigration: Move scripts/check-docs.sh to .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh by hand (git mv preserves history and any local edits), then stamp artifact schema 3 in .materia/project.json. Then update any consumer references that still name the old path: the MATERIA.md § Gate row, package scripts (package.json), CI workflows, settings allowlists, and present-state docs (the dated run folders under docs/specs/ and docs/bugs/_reports/ are frozen and exempt). Or run /materia:migrate --apply, which relocates via the install-check-docs migration (rename in place, then stamp) and surfaces those stale references (referenceFollowUps) for its skill to sweep before re-running the check:docs gate. If the script is absent at BOTH locations, this item does not apply — 0.3.0-check-docs-sh-gate covers installing it (same migration).
changes.notes: The old root scripts/check-docs.sh location keeps working: skills no longer hardcode the gate path — they resolve check:docs from the installed repo's own MATERIA.md § Gate row, so a mixed-vintage install whose § Gate still names the root path runs fine. Relocation is recommended (tidiness + the canonical-layout contract), not required. Shares the install-check-docs migration with 0.3.0-check-docs-sh-gate (buildPlan dedups it). Consumer-staleness lesson (gymii, the first real migration): relocating the artifact cleanly still left the repo's OWN consumers of the old path stale — a broken check:docs gate behind a healthy doctor, because doctor certifies the artifact, not its references. migrate now runs a deterministic reference scan and reports the stale consumers (referenceFollowUps); its skill sweeps the auto-fixable ones (never the frozen dated run folders) and re-runs the gate.

changes.summary: MATERIA.md § Forge gains a merge-strategy knob (the PR-merge method the pipelines use).
changes.detectionNotes: The knob is optional prose inside MATERIA.md § Forge; its absence is indistinguishable from a repo that deliberately omits it, and no default is wrong — so no doctor check can honestly flag it as drift.
changes.manualMigration: Add the merge-strategy knob to MATERIA.md § Forge by hand, matching the current scaffold's § Forge subsection.

changes.summary: The bundled scaffold queue READMEs (proposed-specs / bug-reports) gained clarifying prose.
changes.detectionNotes: A prose refinement inside materialized queue README docs, which the user repo freely edits; there is no canonical byte-string to diff against, so no doctor check can detect it without false positives.

changes.summary: MATERIA.md gains a § Design tool section — the capability seam (author/read/export/tokens/reference) for external design tools the pipeline authors on over MCP; defaults to none.
changes.detectionNotes: A new named prose section in MATERIA.md, which the user repo freely edits; skills that consume the section treat a missing section as none, so absence is behaviorally identical to the shipped default and cannot honestly be flagged as drift.
changes.manualMigration: Add the § Design tool section to MATERIA.md by hand, matching the current scaffold's section (heading, slots, capability meanings, degradation rules); set Tool to none unless the repo actually connects a design tool over MCP.
changes.notes: Nothing consumes the section at this plugin version — it is the seam the later design-stage releases gate on (the consume-missing-as-none rule is a prospective contract on those consumers, not a description of current behavior). Adding it ahead of need is deliberate: later releases edit a known home instead of inventing one. Known discoverability limitation, shared with this file's other optional entries: inspect() surfaces optional changes only inside a schema-adoption window, so a schema-current repo sees this entry in neither doctor nor migrate output — until that gap is closed, it is discoverable only by reading this version file.

changes.summary: The scaffold review-angles README gained a short cross-reference to the reviewer-calibration guidance the ship-spec review machinery provides centrally (severity rubric, structured finding shape, verify-before-reporting).
changes.detectionNotes: A single cross-reference line in a materialized doc the user repo may edit; its absence is not mechanically distinguishable from an intentional local edit, so no doctor check can flag it honestly.

changes.summary: The proposed-spec queue frontmatter gains an optional `surfaces:` field (values drawn from the vocabulary `ui`/`data`, or the empty list `[]`; absent = unknown) and its `schema_version` bumps 1→2; strictly additive (old proposals stay valid).
changes.detectionNotes: Doctor's inspect() does not scan docs/specs/** / the queue README / status-template prose; an old repo's absence of the field is indistinguishable from a user edit, and consumers treat an absent surfaces: / older schema_version as unknown-and-degrade (behaviorally identical to the shipped default) — so no doctor check can honestly flag it.
changes.manualMigration: Update the two materialized scaffold docs to match the current scaffold: add the surfaces: field to _proposed/README.md § Frontmatter contract + Field roles and bump its example schema_version to 2, and add the Surfaces: / ui-surface (predictive): § Notes convention lines to the status template. (Producer emission of the field lives in the plugin skills, not in the repo. No installed-artifact relocation, so /materia:migrate has nothing mechanical to do.)
changes.notes: Shares the same inspect() optional-entry discoverability limitation the sibling optional entries record: a schema-current repo sees this entry in neither doctor nor migrate output — discoverable only by reading this version file. Distinct from 0.3.0-queue-readme-prose: that entry is clarifying prose on the same README; this one is a frontmatter shape + schema_version change. The queue schema_version is its own axis, unrelated to this file's artifactSchema (unchanged at 3).

changes.summary: Ship-spec gains a human design-review gate after the design stage: interactive design-bearing runs pause for approve/revise/abandon, design.md gains an orchestrator-owned `approval:` frontmatter block (pending/approved/auto-approved/abandoned + rounds + body-only design_hash), --auto auto-approves with a recorded stamp, and --approve-design arms a one-shot bypass; default on, disableable per the precedence chain (flag > proposal frontmatter > MATERIA.md § Design tool).
changes.detectionNotes: A per-run product output (design.md's approval-frontmatter contract), not an installed-artifact contract change — per release/README.md § Per-run outputs are outside the contract, its absence is legitimate: a repo may not have run a design-bearing spec since upgrading. Doctor's inspect() never scans docs/specs/**, so the gate state cannot be diffed there either.
changes.manualMigration: Update the materialized scaffold docs to match the current scaffold: docs/specs/_templates/status.md (the two Next: waiting states — design-approval (awaiting operator) / design-abandoned (parked) — and the seven design-gate: § Notes convention lines, plus the § Stages canonical-note extension and its forward-compat default), docs/specs/_templates/design.md (the approval-block contract comment and the Feedback log documentation), docs/specs/_templates/retro.md (the design-gate rounds/category signal), MATERIA.md § Design tool (the Design gate knob line and its precedence chain), docs/standards/skills.md (§ The --approve-design argument), and docs/specs/README.md + CLAUDE.md (the interactive-pause clauses on the runs-to-PR claims). The gate behavior itself lives in the plugin skills and updates with the plugin — no repo action needed for that part.
changes.notes: The `doctor` surfaces token is deliberately omitted even though doctor.mjs gained a design-gate reporting layer: the glossary meaning of `doctor` is "a doctor check applies" (detects this change's drift), and this layer is explicitly read-only, out-of-contract, and carries no stable check id — it never affects status or exit. `validator` is listed because validate-plugin.mjs gained §7b negative-invariant guards for that layer (precedent: 0.3.0-scripts-relocation, which lists `validator` whenever the validator's expectations change). Shares the same inspect() optional-entry discoverability limitation the sibling optional entries record. Pre-gate design.md files (no approval block) are legitimate and are treated as pre-gate runs on resume — today's behavior, not drift. Downstream status note: as the ledger's first recommended + detectable:false entry it is never filterable as adopted, so a schema-behind install (e.g. adopted-but-unstamped) truthfully reports doctor status warnings (exit unchanged at 0) until it stamps to the current schema; a stamped schema-3 repo sees this entry in neither doctor nor migrate output.

changes.summary: The proposed-spec queue frontmatter gains an optional `design_gate: on | off` field (absent = unset, falls through the precedence chain) and its `schema_version` bumps 2→3; strictly additive (v1/v2 proposals stay valid).
changes.detectionNotes: Doctor's inspect() does not scan docs/specs/** / the queue README / status-template prose; an old repo's absence of the field is indistinguishable from a user edit, and consumers treat an absent design_gate: / older schema_version as unset-and-fall-through (behaviorally identical to the shipped default) — so no doctor check can honestly flag it.
changes.manualMigration: Update the materialized scaffold doc to match the current scaffold: add the design_gate: field to _proposed/README.md § Frontmatter contract + Field roles and bump its example schema_version to 3. (Producer emission of the field lives in the plugin skills, not in the repo. No installed-artifact relocation, so /materia:migrate has nothing mechanical to do.)
changes.notes: Shares the same inspect() optional-entry discoverability limitation the sibling optional entries record: a schema-current repo sees this entry in neither doctor nor migrate output — discoverable only by reading this version file. Distinct from 0.3.0-design-review-gate: that entry is the gate behavior + design.md run-artifact contract; this one is the proposed-spec queue-contract shape change. The queue schema_version is its own axis, unrelated to this file's artifactSchema (unchanged at 3).

changes.summary: The design stage authors on the design tool's canvas when an author-capable adapter is configured (design.md becomes the descriptive half of a paired artifact, with canvas: reference/version frontmatter keys); the design template gains a mandatory-on-UI-runs ## Assertions checklist, a per-screen Hierarchy column, and the n/a-with-reason states escape plus domain-specific states; the design gate gains a live canvas-link presentation rung, a direct-canvas-edit revision channel, and a gate-arrival sync; architecture gains a bounded (≤2) design-revision-requested bounce through the gate.
changes.detectionNotes: Per-run product outputs (design.md's extended format, its canvas: frontmatter keys) sit outside the artifact contract — per release/README.md § Per-run outputs are outside the contract, their absence is legitimate: a repo may not have run a design-bearing spec since upgrading, and its adapter may lack the capabilities the canvas pieces gate on. MATERIA.md § Design tool's new budget/detection/drift prose and the status template's bounce § Notes convention are hand-reconciled prose in files the user repo freely edits — no doctor check can honestly flag their absence.
changes.manualMigration: Update the materialized scaffold docs to match the current scaffold: docs/specs/_templates/design.md (the ## Assertions section + its lane-steering comment, the Hierarchy column and n/a-with-reason states relaxation in ## Screens & states, and the canvas: reference/version frontmatter contract with single-block merge semantics), MATERIA.md § Design tool (the Authoring budget bullet, the Canvas-change detection convention, and the Post-approval drift statement), and docs/specs/_templates/status.md (the design-revision (architecture) § Notes convention for the ≤2 bounce bound). The authoring/sync/bounce behavior itself lives in the plugin skills and updates with the plugin — no repo action needed for that part.
changes.notes: Classified recommended (operator decision at intake): the ## Assertions block is load-bearing for downstream design-conformance checking, but existing per-run design.md files keep working untouched. Budget posture: canvas authoring invalidates prior token-budget assumptions for ship-spec runs — one reported Claude Design session consumed more than half a weekly Pro allotment, and a single spec can author on the canvas up to 4 times total (initial + up to 3 revision rounds, any channel, of which at most 2 may be architecture bounces) — MATERIA.md § Design tool's Authoring budget bullet is the operator-facing statement. The committed-snapshot/export machinery and the static design-conformance checker are later releases; this entry covers only the seams they land in. Shares the inspect() discoverability limitation the sibling recommended + detectable:false entry (0.3.0-design-review-gate) records.

changes.summary: The design stage gains a committed static canvas snapshot at docs/specs/<dated-slug>/design/ (self-contained HTML/CSS/assets, exported or reconstructed via read, guarded by a git check-ignore failure), paired with a new fabrication-contract README template; the design gate's Gate arrival and Gate-arrival sync seams are filled with concrete commit-together and re-export mechanics, the Presentation ladder's committed-snapshot rung becomes real, and ship-spec gains a generic rule for a spawned stage's Blocker hand-off to STATUS.md.
changes.detectionNotes: A per-run product output (the design/ directory and its README) sits outside the artifact contract — per release/README.md § Per-run outputs are outside the contract, its absence is legitimate: the configured adapter may lack export/read-reconstruction, or the repo may not have run a design-bearing spec since upgrading. Doctor's inspect() never scans docs/specs/**, so snapshot presence cannot be diffed there either.
changes.manualMigration: Update the materialized scaffold docs to match the current scaffold: docs/specs/_templates/design-snapshot-readme.md (new template — the fabrication-contract categories fabricated/faithful/semantic-structure and the export: via-read honesty clause), docs/specs/README.md (the conditional design/ line in the per-run folder tree and the pipeline table's row 2 conditionality note), and MATERIA.md § Design tool (the export capability bullet's snapshot-path pointer and the Authoring budget bullet's reconstruction-read-cost sentence). The export/commit/sync/presentation behavior itself lives in the plugin skills and updates with the plugin — no repo action needed for that part.
changes.notes: Shares the inspect() discoverability limitation the sibling recommended + detectable:false entry (0.3.0-canvas-authoring) records. Classified recommended: the committed snapshot is load-bearing for a later release's deterministic-diff conformance harness, which scopes its comparison by this entry's design-snapshot-readme.md template's fabrication contract (fabricated / faithful / semantic-structure categories) — existing per-run design.md files and adapters without export/read keep working untouched. Closes out 0.3.0-canvas-authoring's own note that 'the committed-snapshot/export machinery and the static design-conformance checker are later releases; this entry covers only the seams they land in': this entry is that later release landing the committed-snapshot/export half (the static design-conformance checker itself remains a further later release).

changes.summary: Lands the static design-conformance review angle (the gated seventh canonical review angle, opus/high) and its deterministic § Eyes conformance harness: the harness computes prototype-vs-implementation facts scoped by the design snapshot's fabrication contract (faithful vs fabricated, the semantic-structure gate) and emits findings the orchestrator inlines into the angle brief; the angle walks each design.md assertion pass/fail/not-checkable, classifying every discrepancy as implementation-drift or design-debt via a new optional finding-schema classification field; § Loop on findings excludes classified findings from the fix loop and the convergence check, the orchestrator folds them into the review retro entry (new design-drift categories) and banners material design-debt into design.md via § Course corrections; ship-spec § Review runs the harness in the behavioral-verify lane before the fan-out, with the markdown-only/trivial-diff collapses dropping the artifact-based angle.
changes.detectionNotes: The review-angle library (.materia/review-angles/) is a forkable, hand-reconciled artifact the user repo freely edits — doctor's inspect() never scans it, and a missing angle file is not mechanically distinguishable from a deliberate local removal (a gated angle whose file is absent takes ship-spec § Review's missing-file skip-and-record path, not a doctor check). The harness output and its findings are per-run diagnostics outside the artifact contract — per release/README.md § Per-run outputs are outside the contract, their absence is legitimate. The MATERIA.md § Eyes/§ Review prose is hand-reconciled in files the user repo edits — no doctor check can honestly flag its absence.
changes.manualMigration: Update the materialized scaffold to match the current scaffold: .materia/review-angles/design-conformance.md (new angle file — the seventh canonical angle, mission-only body), .materia/review-angles/ui.md (the ui↔design-conformance seam paragraph), MATERIA.md § Eyes (the Design conformance harness subsection) and § Review angles (the design-conformance/design-bearing registry row, the design-bearing gate-token explanation, and the canonical six→seven count), docs/specs/_templates/retro.md (the design-conformance drift categories), and docs/specs/README.md (design-conformance in the seed-angle enumeration). The angle, harness, § Loop dispositions, § Review wiring, and § Course corrections banner behavior live in the plugin skills (ship-spec) and update with the plugin — no repo action needed for that part.
changes.notes: Shares the inspect() optional/recommended-entry discoverability limitation the sibling recommended + detectable:false entries (0.3.0-design-review-gate, 0.3.0-canvas-authoring, 0.3.0-design-snapshot-export) record: a stamped schema-3 repo sees this entry in neither doctor nor migrate output. No artifactSchema bump — the review-angle library and MATERIA.md § Eyes/§ Review prose are hand-reconciled and sit outside the tracked installed-artifact contract (release/README.md). The `ledger` surface token is deliberately omitted: appending a Change entry is not a change to ledger structure/machinery (the 0.3.0 convention — sibling entries that merely append do not self-list `ledger`). Classified recommended (operator decision at intake, opus/high tier): a repo already emitting design.md assertions and committed snapshots gains nothing without the angle — a real capability loss, not a graceful degrade-to-default — while repos without a design-bearing run, or forks that removed the angle, keep working untouched. Closes out 0.3.0-canvas-authoring's and 0.3.0-design-snapshot-export's forecast that 'the static design-conformance checker itself remains a further later release': this entry is that release, landing the checker (harness + angle) whose comparison the design-snapshot-readme.md fabrication contract scopes.
